differential_diagnoses: Plasmodium falciparum malaria (despite negative rapid test)
Septic shock (secondary to translocation or secondary bacterial infection)
next_steps: Perform peripheral blood smear for thick and thin malaria parasite evaluation
Order serologic testing for dengue (NS1 antigen, IgM, IgG) and chikungunya
Send serum and urine samples for leptospirosis PCR/MAT
Order comprehensive viral myocarditis workup (echocardiogram, cardiac enzymes, viral PCR)
Maintain hemodynamic support and monitor for bleeding diathesis (common in dengue/leptospirosis)
Monitor for multi-organ failure and provide supportive care in ICU
Consider diagnostic paracentesis and thoracentesis to analyze fluids for etiology

differential_diagnoses: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis / Secondary septic shock
next_steps: Urgent diagnostic paracentesis (cell count, differential, culture, and albumin for SAAG)
Peripheral blood smear for thick and thin malaria evaluation
Serologic testing for dengue (NS1 antigen, IgM, IgG) and chikungunya
Formal echocardiography to differentiate distributive from cardiogenic shock
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage until SBP/sepsis is ruled out
Serial monitoring of lactate, troponin, and coagulation profile
Evaluation for occult hemorrhage given the history of esophageal varices and thrombocytopenia
